title: Synchronization of listen before talk back-off for synchronous access in unlicensed spectrum
abstract: Synchronization of listen before talk (LBT) back-off for synchronous access in unlicensed spectrum is disclosed. For nodes of a coordinated group of nodes, when one or more such nodes detects a failed LBT attempt, it pauses the contention widow countdown at the current counter value. According to such aspects, a procedure may be provided that specifies when such nodes can restart the contention window countdown using the current counter value or when the nodes can redraw the pseudo-random number for the contention window countdown in order to synchronize the contention window counters.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method of wireless communication, comprising: detecting, by a transmitting node of a plurality of nodes configured into a coordinated group of nodes, neighboring signals occupying a shared communication channel prior to completion of a contention window back-off countdown for a listen before talk (LBT) procedure of the transmitting node; pausing, by the transmitting node, the contention window back-off countdown at a current contention window counter value in response to detection of the neighboring signals; identifying, by the transmitting node, a next synchronization boundary after detection of the neighboring signals; and electing, by the transmitting node, to restart the contention window back-off countdown at the next synchronization boundary, wherein the restart is associated with a synchronization of contention window back-off with remaining nodes of the coordinated group of nodes. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the electing to restart the contention window back-off countdown includes: identifying the expected pseudo-random contention window value; determining a likelihood of one or more neighboring nodes, with use of the expected pseudo-random contention window value, blocking success of the LBT procedure of one or more nodes of the plurality of nodes of the coordinated group of nodes; identifying the restart fails to indicate the synchronization of the contention window back-off in response to the likelihood exceeding a threshold value; and identifying the restart indicates the synchronization of the contention window back off in response to the likelihood within the threshold value. 3. The method of claim 2, further including: refraining from the restarting the contention window back-off counter at the next synchronization boundary in response to failure of the restart to indicate synchronization of the contention window back-off. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the electing to restart the contention window back-off countdown includes: 

 comparing the current contention window counter value to an average value of a current contention window size; redrawing a new pseudo-random contention window value in response to the average value exceeding the current contention window counter value; and restarting the contention window back-off countdown using one of: the new pseudo-random contention window value in response to the redrawing, or the current contention window counter value in response to the current contention window counter value equaling or exceeding the average value. 5. The method of claim 4, further including: semi -statically selecting, by the transmitting node, to one of: activate or deactivate the comparing, the redrawing, and the restarting the contention window back-off countdown, wherein the semi-static selection occurs no more than once every predetermined threshold of time. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein the electing to restart the contention window back-off countdown includes: calculating an average sensing time experienced by the transmitting node for accessing the shared communication channel, wherein the average sensing time is calculated over a long term window; restarting the contention window back-off countdown in response to the average sensing time exceeding a predetermined average sensing time threshold; and refraining from the restart of the contention window back-off countdown in response to the predetermined average sensing time threshold exceeding the average sensing time. 7. An apparatus configured for wireless communication, comprising: means for detecting, by a transmitting node of a plurality of nodes configured into a coordinated group of nodes, neighboring signals occupying a shared communication channel prior to completion of a contention window back-off countdown for a listen before talk (LBT) procedure of the transmitting node; means for pausing, by the transmitting node, the contention window back-off countdown at a current contention window counter value in response to detection of the neighboring signals; 

 means for identifying, by the transmitting node, a next synchronization boundary after detection of the neighboring signals; and means for electing, by the transmitting node, to restart the contention window back off countdown at the next synchronization boundary, wherein the restart is associated with a synchronization of contention window back-off with remaining nodes of the coordinated group of nodes. 8. The apparatus of claim 7, wherein the means for electing to restart the contention window back-off countdown includes: means for identifying the expected pseudo-random contention window value; means for determining a likelihood of one or more neighboring nodes, with use of the expected pseudo-random contention window value, blocking success of the LBT procedure of one or more nodes of the plurality of nodes of the coordinated group of nodes; means for identifying the restart fails to indicate the synchronization of the contention window back-off in response to the likelihood exceeding a threshold value; and means for identifying the restart indicates the synchronization of the contention window back-off in response to the likelihood within the threshold value. 9. The apparatus of claim 8, further including: means for refraining from the restarting the contention window back-off counter at the next synchronization boundary in response to failure of the restart to indicate synchronization of the contention window back-off. 10. The apparatus of claim 7, wherein the means for electing to restart the contention window back-off countdown includes: means for comparing the current contention window counter value to an average value of a current contention window size; means for redrawing a new pseudo-random contention window value in response to the average value exceeding the current contention window counter value; and means for restarting the contention window back-off countdown using one of: the new pseudo-random contention window value in response to the redrawing, or the current contention window counter value in response to the current contention window counter value equaling or exceeding the average value. 11. The apparatus of claim 10, further including: means for semi-statically selecting, by the transmitting node, to one of: activate or deactivate the means for comparing, the means for redrawing, and the means for restarting the contention window back-off countdown, wherein the semi-static selection occurs no more than once every predetermined threshold of time. 12. The apparatus of claim 7, wherein the means for electing to restart the contention window back-off countdown includes: means for calculating an average sensing time experienced by the transmitting node for accessing the shared communication channel, wherein the average sensing time is calculated over a long term window; means for restarting the contention window back-off countdown in response to the average sensing time exceeding a predetermined average sensing time threshold; and means for refraining from the restart of the contention window back-off countdown in response to the predetermined average sensing time threshold exceeding the average sensing time. 13. A non-transitory computer-readable medium having program code recorded thereon, the program code comprising: program code executable by a computer for causing the computer to detect, by a transmitting node of a plurality of nodes configured into a coordinated group of nodes, neighboring signals occupying a shared communication channel prior to completion of a contention window back-off countdown for a listen before talk (LBT) procedure of the transmitting node; program code executable by the computer for causing the computer to pause, by the transmitting node, the contention window back-off countdown at a current contention window counter value in response to detection of the neighboring signals; program code executable by the computer for causing the computer to identify, by the transmitting node, a next synchronization boundary after detection of the neighboring signals; and program code executable by the computer for causing the computer to elect, by the transmitting node, to restart the contention window back-off countdown at the next 

synchronization boundary, wherein the restart is associated with a synchronization of contention window back-off with remaining nodes of the coordinated group of nodes. 14. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 13, wherein the program code executable by the computer for causing the computer to elect to restart the contention window back-off countdown includes: program code executable by the computer for causing the computer to identify the expected pseudo-random contention window value; program code executable by the computer for causing the computer to determine a likelihood of one or more neighboring nodes, with use of the expected pseudo-random contention window value, blocking success of the LBT procedure of one or more nodes of the plurality of nodes of the coordinated group of nodes; program code executable by the computer for causing the computer to identify the restart fails to indicate the synchronization of the contention window back-off in response to the likelihood exceeding a threshold value; and program code executable by the computer for causing the computer to identify the restart indicates the synchronization of the contention window back-off in response to the likelihood within the threshold value. 15. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 14, further including: program code executable by the computer for causing the computer to refrain from the restarting the contention window back-off counter at the next synchronization boundary in response to failure of the restart to indicate synchronization of the contention window back-off. 16. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 13, wherein the program code executable by the computer for causing the computer to elect to restart the contention window back-off countdown includes: program code executable by the computer for causing the computer to compare the current contention window counter value to an average value of a current contention window size; program code executable by the computer for causing the computer to redraw a new pseudo-random contention window value in response to the average value exceeding the current contention window counter value; and 

 program code executable by the computer for causing the computer to restart the contention window back-off countdown using one of: the new pseudo-random contention window value in response to the redrawing, or the current contention window counter value in response to the current contention window counter value equaling or exceeding the average value. 17. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 16, further including: program code executable by the computer for causing the computer to semi-statically select, by the transmitting node, to one of: activate or deactivate the program code executable by the computer for causing the computer to compare, the program code executable by the computer for causing the computer to redraw, and the program code executable by the computer for causing the computer to restart the contention window back-off countdown, wherein the semi-static selection occurs no more than once every predetermined threshold of time. 18. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 15, wherein the program code executable by the computer for causing the computer to elect to restart the contention window back-off countdown includes: program code executable by the computer for causing the computer to calculate an average sensing time experienced by the transmitting node for accessing the shared communication channel, wherein the average sensing time is calculated over a long term window; program code executable by the computer for causing the computer to restart the contention window back-off countdown in response to the average sensing time exceeding a predetermined average sensing time threshold; and program code executable by the computer for causing the computer to refrain from the restart of the contention window back-off countdown in response to the predetermined average sensing time threshold exceeding the average sensing time. 19. An apparatus configured for wireless communication, the apparatus comprising: at least one processor; and a memory coupled to the at least one processor, 

 wherein the at least one processor is configured: to detect, by a transmitting node of a plurality of nodes configured into a coordinated group of nodes, neighboring signals occupying a shared communication channel prior to completion of a contention window back-off countdown for a listen before talk (LBT) procedure of the transmitting node; to pause, by the transmitting node, the contention window back-off countdown at a current contention window counter value in response to detection of the neighboring signals; to identify, by the transmitting node, a next synchronization boundary after detection of the neighboring signals; and to elect, by the transmitting node, to restart the contention window back-off countdown at the next synchronization boundary, wherein the restart is associated with a synchronization of contention window back-off with remaining nodes of the coordinated group of nodes. 20. The apparatus of claim 19, wherein the configuration of the at least one processor to elect to restart the contention window back-off countdown includes configuration of the at least one processor: to identify the expected pseudo-random contention window value; to determine a likelihood of one or more neighboring nodes, with use of the expected pseudo-random contention window value, blocking success of the LBT procedure of one or more nodes of the plurality of nodes of the coordinated group of nodes; to identify the restart fails to indicate the synchronization of the contention window back-off in response to the likelihood exceeding a threshold value; and to identify the restart indicates the synchronization of the contention window back-off in response to the likelihood within the threshold value. 21. The apparatus of claim 20, further including configuration of the at least one processor to refrain from the restarting the contention window back-off counter at the next synchronization boundary in response to failure of the restart to indicate synchronization of the contention window back-off 22. The apparatus of claim 19, wherein the configuration of the at least one processor to elect to restart the contention window back-off countdown includes configuration of the at least one processor: to compare the current contention window counter value to an average value of a current contention window size; to redraw a new pseudo-random contention window value in response to the average value exceeding the current contention window counter value; and to restart the contention window back-off countdown using one of: the new pseudo-random contention window value in response to the redrawing, or the current contention window counter value in response to the current contention window counter value equaling or exceeding the average value. 23. The apparatus of claim 23, further including configuration of the at least one processor to semi-statically select, by the transmitting node, one of: activation or deactivation of the configuration of the at least one processor to compare, the configuration of the at least one processor to redraw, and the configuration of the at least one processor to restart the contention window back-off countdown, wherein the semi-static selection occurs no more than once every predetermined threshold of time. 24. The apparatus medium of claim 20, wherein the configuration of the at least one processor to elect to restart the contention window back-off countdown includes configuration of the at least one processor: to calculate an average sensing time experienced by the transmitting node for accessing the shared communication channel, wherein the average sensing time is calculated over a long term window; to restart the contention window back-off countdown in response to the average sensing time exceeding a predetermined average sensing time threshold; and to refrain from the restart of the contention window back-off countdown in response to the predetermined average sensing time threshold exceeding the average sensing time.
